Dentate thickness difference — %
Dentate gyrus thickness measurement:
Measure the thickest portion of the dentate gyrus – CA4 at the hippocampal head in the coronal plane.

Coronal MRI: dentate gyrus–CA4 thickness measurement on both hippocampi (L_1 and L_2)

🔺

Hippocampal Head Coronal Angle

Enter the coronal angle of each hippocampus in degrees

°
°
Angular difference — %
Hippocampal coronal angle measurement:
Measure the angle of the thickest hippocampal head in the coronal plane (verify in the sagittal plane).

• Place the vertex of the angle at the nearest ventricular wall, aligned with the axis of the hippocampal head in that plane.
• Place the ends of the angle at the midpoint of both the superior and inferior aspects of the hippocampal head, always in the coronal plane.

Sagittal MRI: identification of the thickest hippocampal head

Coronal MRI: hippocampal head angle measurement (A: 39.0°)

Equation: logit(p) = −26.55 + 0.812×DiffVol + 1.128×DiffDentate + 0.230×DiffAngle

📊 Methodological Summary

This model was built through the following steps:

  • Design: Observational case-control study. n = 40 patients (20 with hippocampal sclerosis, 20 controls), after excluding 6 cases with incomplete angle/thickness data.
  • Univariate analysis: Mann-Whitney U test for continuous variables. Percentage differences between hippocampi were assessed for volume, dentate thickness, CA1 thickness, elongation, FLAIR intensity, and coronal angle.
  • Variable selection: Three variables with the highest discrimination and complete data were included: hippocampal volume difference (p < 0.0001), dentate gyrus thickness difference (p < 0.0001), and coronal angle difference (p < 0.0001). Excluded: FLAIR intensity (35% missing), CA1 thickness (correlated with dentate, r = 0.33), elongation (weak discrimination), and gyri morphology (p = 1.0).
  • Model: Binary logistic regression with L2 regularization (ridge, C = 5), required due to near-perfect data separation in the small sample.
  • Validation: Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation (LOO-CV). AUC-LOO = 0.995 (95% CI: 0.977–1.000). Bootstrap optimism (2,000 resamples): 0.005. Brier Score LOO = 0.055. Hosmer-Lemeshow p = 0.88 (good fit).
  • Diagnostic performance (LOO-CV): Sensitivity = 100%, Specificity = 95%, PPV = 95.2%, NPV = 100%, Accuracy = 97.5%, Youden Index = 0.95.
  • Probability thresholds: Low (< 5.3%, Youden optimal cutoff) · Moderate (5.3%–70%) · High (> 70%).
logit(p) = −26.55 + 0.812 × DiffVol + 1.128 × DiffDentate + 0.230 × DiffAngle

The inter-side difference is computed as: |larger − smaller| / larger × 100. Final probability: p = 1 / (1 + e−logit).

⚠️ Important Notice

This calculator is for exploratory use only and is part of an ongoing research project. It must not be used for clinical purposes or to inform diagnostic or therapeutic decisions in real patients. Results are statistical estimates generated from a small sample (n = 40) and have not yet been externally validated. This tool was developed under the scientific direction of Dr. Doriam Perera, functional neurosurgeon, within a neurosurgical research project. Any interpretation must be made by qualified healthcare professionals within an appropriate clinical context.
